{{Quote|I am doctor Xathan, archaeologist, and this is my aide Fem Nu-Ar. And you are…?|Xathan, upon meeting Han Solo|<ref name="SW84" />}}
[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Ualp Xathan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Archaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologist]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Ualp Xathan was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]]<ref name="SW84" /> at some point before [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]].<ref name="Birth" /> He made a career in the field of [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeology]]. In [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]], Xathan authored ''[[One Thousand, One Hundred and Thirty-Eight Wonders of the Ancient Galaxy]]'', which was published on [[Beshka]] by [[TriPlanetary Press/Legends|TriPlanetary Press]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|Ten years later]], the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash Arranda]] consulted that book while redacting an [[Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd, Or: What I Did On My Inter-Term Break|essay]] recounting a visit she made to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]] with her adopted uncle [[Mammon Hoole]].<ref name="SPL">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20090531/index.html?page=1|text=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd: A Hyperspace Exclusive|int=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd}}</ref> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Xathan and his assistant [[Fem Nu-Ar]] were hired by the Empire to find the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Seoul 5]]. They eventually found the planet, which was once the home of an ancient race of [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. But when Xathan and Nu-Ar brought the Imperials to the planet, they were imprisoned. They were later rescued by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].<ref name="SW84" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Young man, I demand to see what you have in that bag!|Xathan, upon meeting Han Solo|<ref name="SW84" />}}
As a reptiloid, Xathan was tall, with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He often addressed others in a slightly patronizing yet endearing tone, calling them "my dear," "m'lad" or "m'boy."<ref name="SW84" />
